reasoning: The 10-year term premium recently experienced a sharp spike, reaching 0.6161 on April 11, 2025. This value is significantly above the linear trend and historical averages over the last year. Statistical analysis from the FRED tool indicates a 29% chance of it increasing further by April 20, leaning heavily towards mean reversion in the short term. Because term premiums are bounded and tend to mean-revert over time, the probability of exceeding this high anchor (0.6161) remains below 50% across the forecast horizon. Following the random walk and increasing uncertainty principles, the probability shifts gradually closer to 0.50 (maximum uncertainty) for dates further in the future: 0.33 for April 20, 0.38 for May 13, 0.43 for July 12, and 0.48 for October 10.
